decision: WorldEvent mutual-exclusion tiebreak is alphabetical (id.localeCompare) when same year — deterministic but author-invisible. wr_autocratic always beats wr_utopian at 2045. Acceptable for now; add priority field if gameplay needs authored ordering.
why: Code reviewer flagged; not a bug, a design tradeoff. Determinism is the priority for replay correctness.

subject: feat(ui): HUD polish + pole-gate threshold fix (de-ui-c)
decision: Pole-fork world-event ideology gates raised ±30→±40 as a tuning improvement. Raw ideology (25% of composite tyrannyUtopiaAxis) and the composite axis are deliberately different signals: world events read raw political lean; moralPoleOf reads composite personality. The gates are not intended to be numerically identical — the commit message was misleading. No code change needed; behavior is correct (stricter personality gate = less accidental pole divergence in moderate-ideology runs).
why: Review finding clarified: the two breakpoints (raw ideology axis vs composite tyrannyUtopiaAxis) are different scales by design. No schema support for gating on composite axis. ±40 raw ideology is a reasonable stricter threshold independent of the ±40 composite axis.
